steps.title: Recognize the same line
steps.content: The second equation is $5$ times the first: $$5(2x+3y=7) gives 10x+15y=35.$$ So a point only needs to satisfy $2x+3y=7$ to lie on both graphs.

steps.title: Use r as the y-value
steps.content: The answer choices are written in terms of $r$. Let $y=r$ and solve the first equation for $x$: $$2x+3r=7$$ $$2x=7-3r$$ $$x=-3r/2+7/2$$

steps.title: Match the point
steps.content: The point is therefore $(-3r/2+7/2,r)$, which matches choice B.
